competitors.name: You
competitors.rules: Your real Robinhood account, rebased to the shared origin so it's comparable. Performance only — holdings and trades are deliberately not published.

competitors.name: congress_mirror
competitors.rules: Mirrors the most successful members of Congress: ranks filers by disclosed-trade excess return (kadoa STOCK Act mirror), then buys the names the top 10 disclosed purchasing within 120 days, weighted by how many bought each. Traded on the disclosure date — weeks after their actual fill.

competitors.name: momentum_breakout
competitors.rules: Buy a 20-day-high breakout confirmed by above-average volume; exit when price closes below its 20-day SMA. Few big winners, many small losers.

trade_log.reason: fill to cap: breakout > 20d high on volume
fill to cap: breakout > 20d high on volume

competitors.name: blended_momo_rsi
competitors.rules: Momentum breakout, but skip entries already overbought (RSI ≥ 70); exit on trend break or RSI > 75. Avoids chasing extended moves.

trade_log.reason: fill to cap: breakout, RSI 64 not overbought
fill to cap: breakout, RSI 66 not overbought

competitors.name: mean_reversion
competitors.rules: Buy when RSI(14) < 30 (oversold); sell when RSI(14) > 70 (overbought). Trades less, wins more often, smaller edge.

competitors.name: S&P 500
competitors.rules: Buys SPY with the whole book on the first forward session and holds — the market baseline.

trade_log.reason: buy & hold: all-in on day one

competitors.name: llm_voters
competitors.rules: Allocates across the swarm's top vote-getters (weight ∝ vote share, capped at 60%); rebalanced each tick.

competitors.name: deep_research_analyst
competitors.rules: Holds the analyst's target weights; rebalanced each tick from a fresh research report (web + Robinhood data).

competitors.name: mirofish_real
competitors.rules: Real-MiroFish: persona agents with memory interact over rounds, each ranking its best ideas; the panel's rank-weighted consensus (top 5 names, weight ∝ Borda points) rebalanced each tick.

analyst.framework: Claude for Financial Services — equity-research methodology (/screen, /sector, /comps, /catalysts, /thesis). Data: Robinhood fundamentals + web_search (no paid-vendor MCP connectors).
analyst.sizing: 12% — new defensive-momentum anchor, funded by cutting CAT and trimming MU to lower book beta

regime.label: Hawkish Fed, sticky inflation, near-ATH tape
regime.note: Fed held 3.50–3.75% on 6/17 with a 1–2 hike dot plot and a RAISED inflation outlook (3.6% headline / 3.3% core); 10yr 4.48%, S&P near all-time highs (~7,420), Iran conflict keeps energy/oil an inflation risk. Favors cheap quality cyclicals with pricing power + defensives that carry their own momentum; penalizes long-duration growth at a 4.48% 10yr.

analyst.thesis: What changed since 6/25: the hawkish/inflation regime is now confirmed (Fed held 3.50–3.75% with a 1–2 hike dot plot and a raised 3.6% headline inflation outlook; 10yr 4.48%; S&P near all-time highs; Iran energy risk), so I keep the cyclical-value/quality tilt. But 6/26 exposed the flaw in last tick's book: it was too concentrated in high-beta cyclicals that all fall together — on a −0.57% SPY day, MU −7.5%, CAT −5.6% and JPM −2.1% erased the prior day's MU-earnings alpha with no defensive ballast. So this tick I de-risk while staying regime-aligned: keep the cheap quality anchors (BAC PE 14, JPM PE 16, GE aerospace), trim the volatile winner (MU, banking more of the earnings spike), CUT the priciest rolling-over laggard (CAT, PE 53, −5.6% off its 52-wk high), and INITIATE a defensive-momentum diversifier — UNH, at a fresh 52-wk high on a red day, Optum margin recovery, Q2 catalyst ahead. ~46% cash because the tape sits near all-time highs into a hawkish Fed: asymmetric downside.

evidence.point: Fed held the funds rate at 3.50–3.75% on 6/17; median dots call for 1–2 hikes in 2026; inflation outlook RAISED to 3.6% headline / 3.3% core (was 2.7% in March); May CPI ran 4.2% headline / 2.9% core.
10-year Treasury yield 4.48%; S&P 500 ~7,420 near a new all-time high (6/17 close); Iran conflict keeps oil/energy an active inflation input.

UNH printed a fresh 52-wk high of 427.89 on 6/26 (RH: high_52_weeks 427.89, date 2026-06-26), +2.8% on a −0.57% SPY day, +28% YTD; Wall Street Moderate Buy, Bernstein PT $492 / Leerink $462, citing Optum margin recovery.
BAC trades at PE 14.44, P/B 1.51, 1.89% yield, last 57.81 vs a 52-wk high of 59.195 (6/25) — the cheapest, highest-quality name in the book.
JPM PE 16.05, last 328.08 vs 52-wk high 343.45 (6/25); GE PE 45.77 at a 52-wk high of 379.67 (6/25), Aerospace & Defense quality compounder.
MU reversed hard on 6/26: open 1139.5 → intraday high 1198.71 → close 1122.99 (−7.46%), giving back much of the earnings gap; still +21.6% over 20 sessions, PE 27.47.
CAT is the priciest holding at PE 52.59, P/B 26.1, and fell −5.6% on 6/26 off its 6/25 52-wk high of 1057.07 — rich and rolling over near-term.

MRNA (exited 6/25) ripped +12.7% on 6/26 on a 9-0 FDA adcomm vote for its mFLUSIVA flu vaccine (PDUFA 2026-08-05) and a Science Day pipeline reset — but remains an unprofitable biotech (negative P/E) I won't chase after a ~40% run off the lows.

risks: Concentrated quality-cyclical book is still high-beta to the downside — 6/26 proved a single red day can wipe a day's alpha (MU −7.5%, CAT −5.6%). UNH lowers but does not eliminate this.
BAC/JPM/GE sit near 52-wk highs; a credit-cycle or rates shock, or a dovish Fed surprise that unwinds the value-over-growth tilt, hits the whole sleeve at once.
UNH carries managed-care headline/regulatory risk and a binary Q2 earnings catalyst; chasing a fresh 52-wk high can mean buying near-term top.
MU memory/AI-capex is volatile; a memory-price or hyperscaler-capex wobble would extend today's reversal.

data_examined.label: Whole-universe momentum/relative-strength screen (1d/5d/20d return, 52-wk proximity, RS vs SPY) over shuffled UNIVERSE
Robinhood fundamentals on 8 finalists (PE, P/B, 52-wk range, yield, sector): GE, BAC, JPM, CAT, MU, UNH, DE, VRTX
Macro regime read (Fed 6/17 decision, dot plot, inflation outlook, 10yr, Iran energy)
Catalyst checks: MRNA spike (FDA flu adcomm/Science Day), UNH 52-wk high (Optum margins, Q2)

rationale.BAC: Anchor of the book — the cheapest, highest-quality name I own. [DATA] PE 14.44, P/B 1.51, 1.89% yield, last 57.81 just below a fresh 52-wk high of 59.195 (6/25); held up on the 6/26 red day (−0.7%). [ASSUMED] A hawkish Fed / higher-for-longer rates is a tailwind for net interest income at a well-capitalized money-center bank, and a 14x multiple gives downside support the rest of the book lacks. Sized 0.13.
rationale.GE: Quality aerospace/defense compounder at a 52-wk high. [DATA] At a 52-wk high of 379.67 (6/25), +14.9% over 20 sessions, only −0.8% on the 6/26 red day; PE 45.77, Aerospace & Defense. [ASSUMED] Commercial-aero services + defense budgets are a durable, less rate-sensitive cyclical with pricing power that fits the inflation regime; the rich multiple is the cost of best-in-class momentum, so I keep it at anchor weight (0.13) rather than adding.
rationale.UNH: New initiation — the defensive-momentum diversifier this book was missing. [DATA] Fresh 52-wk high of 427.89 on 6/26 (+2.8% on a −0.57% SPY day), +28% YTD, PE 31.4; Wall Street Moderate Buy with Bernstein $492 / Leerink $462 targets on Optum margin recovery; Q2 earnings is the next catalyst. [ASSUMED] Managed-care demand is non-cyclical, so UNH lowers book beta while still carrying its own uptrend — exactly the ballast that was absent on 6/26. Sized 0.12, funded by cutting CAT.
rationale.MU: Trim, don't abandon. [DATA] Reversed −7.46% on 6/26 (open 1139.5 → high 1198.71 → close 1122.99) after the blowout print, but still +21.6% over 20 sessions; PE 27.47. [ASSUMED] The AI-memory/HBM thesis is intact, but the 6/26 intraday reversal shows how violent the two-way risk is, so I bank more of the spike and cut the position from 0.10 to 0.08 to keep it a satellite, not a core.
rationale.JPM: Cheap quality bank, kept as a smaller second financial. [DATA] PE 16.05, last 328.08 vs 52-wk high 343.45 (6/25); −2.1% on 6/26. [ASSUMED] Same higher-for-longer NII tailwind as BAC, but I keep it smaller (0.08) than BAC to avoid doubling the same major-bank factor; trimmed slightly from 0.10 to make room for UNH.

reflection.looking_back: Last tick I rotated fully into cyclical value — GE 0.14, CAT 0.12, BAC 0.12, MU 0.10, JPM 0.10, ~42% cash — and on 6/25 it beat SPY by +2.1 pts (book +2.1% vs a flat SPY) on the MU earnings gap. But held into 6/26 that same concentrated book gave most of it back: MU −7.5% (reversed intraday from 1198 to a 1123 close), CAT −5.6%, JPM −2.1% on a −0.57% SPY day. All-time the book is still −8.8% and −6.5 pts behind SPY.
worked: GE and BAC, the quality anchors, held up on the 6/26 red day (GE −0.8%, BAC −0.7%) and sit ~0.97 of fresh 52-wk highs — validating the quality-cyclical tilt.
BAC at PE 14.4 is doing its job as the cheapest, lowest-beta name in the book; value gave downside support the high-multiple names didn't.
missed: The book was too high-beta and one-sided: every cyclical sold off together on 6/26 (MU/CAT/JPM), so one down day erased the MU alpha — there was no defensive ballast.
Cut MRNA one day too early — it ripped +12.7% on 6/26 on the FDA flu adcomm (9-0) and Science Day, though it stays an unprofitable biotech I wouldn't re-chase after a ~40% run.
reflection.adjustment: Add a defensive sleeve with its own momentum (initiate UNH, a fresh 52-wk high on a red day) and cut the priciest rolling-over cyclical (CAT, PE 52.6, −5.6% today); keep the cheap quality anchors (BAC, GE, JPM), trim MU to bank more of the spike — lowering book beta while staying regime-aligned.

swarm.note: Live swarm — 150 fish, each a unique trader profile shown its own random ~20-name slice of the watchlist (with recent headlines), voted via OpenRouter. Independent-voter election; dissenters (ringed) chose CASH.

live.note: Connected to the Agentic cash account •••• — currently unfunded ($0). Deposit into this specific account to trade the real $100. No agent orders placed yet.

rules.momentum_breakout: Buy a 20-day-high breakout confirmed by above-average volume; exit when price closes below its 20-day SMA. Few big winners, many small losers.
rules.mean_reversion: Buy when RSI(14) < 30 (oversold); sell when RSI(14) > 70 (overbought). Trades less, wins more often, smaller edge.
rules.blended_momo_rsi: Momentum breakout, but skip entries already overbought (RSI ≥ 70); exit on trend break or RSI > 75. Avoids chasing extended moves.
rules.deep_research_analyst: Holds the analyst's target weights; rebalanced each tick from a fresh research report (web + Robinhood data).
rules.llm_voters: Allocates across the swarm's top vote-getters (weight ∝ vote share, capped at 60%); rebalanced each tick.
rules.mirofish_real: Real-MiroFish: persona agents with memory interact over rounds, each ranking its best ideas; the panel's rank-weighted consensus (top 5 names, weight ∝ Borda points) rebalanced each tick.
rules.congress_mirror: Mirrors the most successful members of Congress: ranks filers by disclosed-trade excess return (kadoa STOCK Act mirror), then buys the names the top 10 disclosed purchasing within 120 days, weighted by how many bought each. Traded on the disclosure date — weeks after their actual fill.

sources.name: Standings, curves, decision trail (LIVE)
sources.detail: The forward paper test — every competitor trades a $100 book the same way, advanced one trading day per tick (rule strategies via tick.py / bot/engine.py; agents via run_agents.py / bot/paper.py), 5 bps slippage, −15% stop. Young — it grows one session per tick.

sources.name: Backtest reference
sources.detail: A separate Dec–Jun walk-forward backtest (2026-01-02–2026-06-26, in-sample, mostly-bull) is shown inside each rule strategy's detail for context only — it is NOT the live board.

sources.name: Research analyst
sources.detail: Agent-driven on the Claude Code plan: web_search + Robinhood data → one report (state/analyst.json) with target weights per tick. Sources link from each evidence row.

sources.name: Swarm vote
sources.detail: Independent-voter election: 150 unique-profile cheap models (DeepSeek/Gemini/Llama/Haiku via OpenRouter) each read one shared briefing and return a single ballot; tallied in bot/swarm.py. No interaction between fish.

sources.name: Real-MiroFish swarm
sources.detail: Social-simulation swarm (bot/mirofish.py): persona agents with memory interact over multiple rounds — each re-ranks its best ideas seeing its own prior view and its neighbours' latest — so a consensus forms via peer influence (the opposite of the independent swarm). The panel's rank-weighted (Borda) consensus becomes a multi-name book (top 5 names, weight ∝ points). Tiered cost (cheap/default/qwen); seeded from the briefing + optional world-events news.

sources.name: Live account
sources.detail: Robinhood MCP, Agentic cash account ••••, via get_portfolio / get_equity_positions / get_equity_orders.

sources.name: Display scale
sources.detail: The real paper books are $100 each; every dollar and share figure on this site is shown ×100 (a $10,000 book) so holdings read in whole shares and dollars. Per-share prices and percentages are unscaled.
